description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that runs a series of container‑scanning jobs using the rpm‑verify‑fips component from gitlab.com/gitlab‑org/cloud‑native/container‑dependencies‑finder. Define a single stage called container‑scanning and for each of the following image prefixes—certificates, cfssl-self-sign, gitaly, gitlab-base, gitlab-container-registry, gitlab-openbao, gitlab-exporter, gitlab-geo-logcursor, gitlab-kas, gitlab-agentk, gitlab-mailroom, gitlab-pages, gitlab-rails-ee, gitlab-ruby, gitlab-shell, gitlab-sidekiq-ee, gitlab-toolbox-ee, gitlab-webservice-ee, gitlab-workhorse-ee, kubectl, gitlab-zoekt—add a job named <prefix>-fips-verify that runs in the container‑scanning stage. Each job is generated by including the rpm‑verify‑fips component with inputs: allow_failure set to true, authenticate_registry set to false, container_image set to ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}/<image>:${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}-fips, and job_prefix set to the same prefix, causing the component to execute its built‑in script that pulls the image and runs the FIPS verification command inside a Docker environment. All jobs inherit the component’s default Docker image, have no extra caches or artifacts, and run on every pipeline without additional rules, only respecting the allow_failure flag. This configuration provides automatic FIPS compliance verification for each GitLab component image built in the CI pipeline.
